statement.en: A confirmatory factor analyses of the order-processing questionnaire: A potential tool for detecting early mathematical difficulties
statement.summary: The Order-Processing Questionnaire (OPQ) is a brief parental-report measure designed to assess children’s everyday ordering skills, which have been linked to early mathematical development. This study aimed to validate the OPQ’s two-factor structure, previously identified by O’Connor et al. (2018), using confirmatory factor analysis (CFA) in a larger sample. Parents of 509 children in their first year of primary school in Northern Ireland completed the OPQ online. CFA tested the original two-factor model comprising positively and negatively worded items. Model fit was mixed: while indices such as the Comparative Fit Index (CFI) and Standardised Root Mean Square Residual (SRMR) indicated adequate fit (i.e. the analysis provides a reasonable representation of the patterns in the data), the R

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Study reports mixed model fit results (CFI/SRMR adequate, but R-squared metrics incomplete in abstract). Confirmatory validation is provisional—awaiting full results, peer review, and replication. The abstract is truncated, preventing complete assessment of empirical claims.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 秩序と混沌の比が、数学の三乗根に、抑制された声の力を掛けたもので表される。
